name: spiderfoot
title: spiderfoot

commands.name: spiderfoot

arguments.description: show this help message and exit
arguments.raw: -h, --help            show this help message and exit

arguments.description: Enable debug output.
arguments.raw: -d, --debug           Enable debug output.

arguments.description: port            IP and port to listen on.
arguments.raw: -l IP:port            IP and port to listen on.

arguments.description: Modules to enable.
arguments.raw: -m mod1,mod2,...      Modules to enable.

arguments.description: List available modules.
arguments.raw: -M, --modules         List available modules.
-C, --correlate scanID

arguments.description: Target for the scan.
arguments.raw: -s TARGET             Target for the scan.

arguments.description: Event types to collect (modules selected
arguments.raw: -t type1,type2,...    Event types to collect (modules selected
-u {all,footprint,investigate,passive}

arguments.description: List available event types.
arguments.raw: -T, --types           List available event types.

arguments.description: Output format. Tab is default.
arguments.raw: -o {tab,csv,json}     Output format. Tab is default.

arguments.description: Don't print field headers, just data.
arguments.raw: -H                    Don't print field headers, just data.

arguments.description: Strip newlines from data.
arguments.raw: -n                    Strip newlines from data.

arguments.description: Include the source data field in tab/csv output.
arguments.raw: -r                    Include the source data field in tab/csv output.

arguments.description: Maximum data length to display. By default, all data
arguments.raw: -S LENGTH             Maximum data length to display. By default, all data

arguments.description: Delimiter to use for CSV output. Default is ,.
arguments.raw: -D DELIMITER          Delimiter to use for CSV output. Default is ,.

arguments.description: Filter out other event types that weren't requested
arguments.raw: -f                    Filter out other event types that weren't requested

arguments.description: Show only a set of event types, comma-separated.
arguments.raw: -F type1,type2,...    Show only a set of event types, comma-separated.

arguments.description: STRICT MODE. Will only enable modules that can
arguments.raw: -x                    STRICT MODE. Will only enable modules that can

arguments.description: Disable logging. This will also hide errors!
arguments.raw: -q                    Disable logging. This will also hide errors!

arguments.description: Display the version of SpiderFoot and exit.
arguments.raw: -V, --version         Display the version of SpiderFoot and exit.
-max-threads MAX_THREADS

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# spiderfoot -h
 usage: sf.py [-h] [-d] [-l IP:port] [-m mod1,mod2,...] [-M] [-C scanID]
            [-s TARGET] [-t type1,type2,...]
            [-u {all,footprint,investigate,passive}] [-T] [-o {tab,csv,json}]
            [-H] [-n] [-r] [-S LENGTH] [-D DELIMITER] [-f]
            [-F type1,type2,...] [-x] [-q] [-V] [-max-threads MAX_THREADS]
 
 SpiderFoot 4.0.0: Open Source Intelligence Automation.
 
 options:
 -h, --help            show this help message and exit
 -d, --debug           Enable debug output.
 -l IP:port            IP and port to listen on.
 -m mod1,mod2,...      Modules to enable.
 -M, --modules         List available modules.
 -C, --correlate scanID
                       Run correlation rules against a scan ID.
 -s TARGET             Target for the scan.
 -t type1,type2,...    Event types to collect (modules selected
                       automatically).
 -u {all,footprint,investigate,passive}
                       Select modules automatically by use case
 -T, --types           List available event types.
 -o {tab,csv,json}     Output format. Tab is default.
 -H                    Don't print field headers, just data.
 -n                    Strip newlines from data.
 -r                    Include the source data field in tab/csv output.
 -S LENGTH             Maximum data length to display. By default, all data
                       is shown.
 -D DELIMITER          Delimiter to use for CSV output. Default is ,.
 -f                    Filter out other event types that weren't requested
                       with -t.
 -F type1,type2,...    Show only a set of event types, comma-separated.
 -x                    STRICT MODE. Will only enable modules that can
                       directly consume your target, and if -t was specified
                       only those events will be consumed by modules. This
                       overrides -t and -m options.
 -q                    Disable logging. This will also hide errors!
 -V, --version         Display the version of SpiderFoot and exit.
 -max-threads MAX_THREADS
                       Max number of modules to run concurrently.

commands.name: spiderfoot-cli

arguments.description: show this help message and exit
arguments.raw: -h, --help   show this help message and exit

arguments.description: Enable debug output.
arguments.raw: -d, --debug  Enable debug output.

arguments.description: Username to authenticate to SpiderFoot server.
arguments.raw: -u USER      Username to authenticate to SpiderFoot server.

arguments.description: Password to authenticate to SpiderFoot server. Consider using
arguments.raw: -p PASS      Password to authenticate to SpiderFoot server. Consider using

arguments.metavar: PASSFILE instead so that your password isn't visible in your
arguments.raw: -P PASSFILE instead so that your password isn't visible in your

arguments.description: Execute commands from FILE.
arguments.raw: -e FILE      Execute commands from FILE.

arguments.description: Log command history to FILE. By default, history is stored to
arguments.raw: -l FILE      Log command history to FILE. By default, history is stored to

arguments.description: Disable history logging.
arguments.raw: -n           Disable history logging.

arguments.description: Spool commands and output to FILE.
arguments.raw: -o FILE      Spool commands and output to FILE.

arguments.description: Allow insecure server connections when using SSL
arguments.raw: -i           Allow insecure server connections when using SSL

arguments.description: Silent output, only errors reported.
arguments.raw: -q           Silent output, only errors reported.

arguments.description: Turn off color-coded output.
arguments.raw: -k           Turn off color-coded output.

arguments.description: Print the banner w/ version and exit.
arguments.raw: -b, -v       Print the banner w/ version and exit.

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# spiderfoot-cli -h
 usage: sfcli.py [-h] [-d] [-s URL] [-u USER] [-p PASS] [-P PASSFILE] [-e FILE]
               [-l FILE] [-n] [-o FILE] [-i] [-q] [-k] [-b]
 
 SpiderFoot: Open Source Intelligence Automation.
 
 options:
 -h, --help   show this help message and exit
 -d, --debug  Enable debug output.
 -s URL       Connect to SpiderFoot server on URL. By default, a connection
              to http://127.0.0.1:5001 will be attempted.
 -u USER      Username to authenticate to SpiderFoot server.
 -p PASS      Password to authenticate to SpiderFoot server. Consider using
              -P PASSFILE instead so that your password isn't visible in your
              shell history or in process lists!
 -P PASSFILE  File containing password to authenticate to SpiderFoot server.
              Ensure permissions on the file are set appropriately!
 -e FILE      Execute commands from FILE.
 -l FILE      Log command history to FILE. By default, history is stored to
              ~/.spiderfoot_history unless disabled with -n.
 -n           Disable history logging.
 -o FILE      Spool commands and output to FILE.
 -i           Allow insecure server connections when using SSL
 -q           Silent output, only errors reported.
 -k           Turn off color-coded output.
 -b, -v       Print the banner w/ version and exit.

local_help.--help:
usage: sfcli.py [-h] [-d] [-s URL] [-u USER] [-p PASS] [-P PASSFILE] [-e FILE]
                [-l FILE] [-n] [-o FILE] [-i] [-q] [-k] [-b]

SpiderFoot: Open Source Intelligence Automation.

  -h, --help   show this help message and exit

               to http://127.0.0.1:5001 will be attempted.

  -p PASS      Password to authenticate to SpiderFoot server. Consider using
               -P PASSFILE instead so that your password isn't visible in your
               shell history or in process lists!
  -P PASSFILE  File containing password to authenticate to SpiderFoot server.
               Ensure permissions on the file are set appropriately!
  -e FILE      Execute commands from FILE.
  -l FILE      Log command history to FILE. By default, history is stored to

  -n           Disable history logging.
  -o FILE      Spool commands and output to FILE.

  -q           Silent output, only errors reported.
  -k           Turn off color-coded output.
  -b, -v       Print the banner w/ version and exit.

binary_metadata.file_type: a sh script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.depends: python3:any, python3-adblockparser, python3-bs4, python3-cherrypy3, python3-cherrypy-cors, python3-cryptography, python3-dnspython, python3-docx, python3-exifread, python3-ipwhois, python3-lxml, python3-mako, python3-netaddr, python3-networkx, python3-openssl, python3-openpyxl, python3-phonenumbers, python3-pptx, python3-pypdf, python3-publicsuffixlist, python3-gexf, python3-requests, python3-secure (>= 1.0.0), python3-socks, python3-whois, python3-yaml
apt_metadata.description:
OSINT collection and reconnaissance tool
This package contains an open source intelligence (OSINT) automation tool. Its
goal is to automate the process of gathering intelligence about a given
target, which may be an IP address, domain name, hostname, network subnet,
ASN, e-mail address or person's name.
.
SpiderFoot can be used offensively, i.e. as part of a black-box penetration
test to gather information about the target, or defensively to identify what
information you or your organisation are freely providing for attackers to use
against you.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
